headline: 8 Rivers Classified as Dead After Industrial Contamination
context: DOE's 2024 National Water Quality Report classified 8 river stretches as Class V — effectively dead — with zero dissolved oxygen, all in industrial zones in Selangor, Johor, and Penang.

cards.big: 8 Malaysian rivers have zero dissolved oxygen. Nothing can live in them.
cards.sub: All 8 are in industrial zones. All 8 have been deteriorating for over a decade with documented non-compliance.

cards.big: DOE issued 1,847 warning letters to polluters on these 8 rivers since 2015.
cards.sub: Warning letters carry no legal penalty. They are administrative gestures.

cards.big: Rehabilitating a Class V river to Class III costs RM12-18M per kilometre.
cards.sub: Total rehabilitation for 8 river stretches: estimated RM2.4B. No allocation exists.
Japan's approach: criminal penalties, mandatory treatment, and polluter-funded restoration.

cards.big: 1,847 warning letters and 8 dead rivers. The warnings are not working. They were never designed to.
Dead rivers are not inevitable. They are the result of a system that warns instead of punishes. Malaysia needs to choose between warning letters and living rivers.
